sourceMovie.title: The Aviator
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Aviator (2004) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Titanic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Aviator clicked for you, Titanic (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Top Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Aviator clicked for you, Top Gun (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Aviator?
faq.answer: Try Titanic, Top Gun, Sully—each shares DNA with The Aviator while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Aviator?
faq.answer: The Aviator (2004) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Aviator worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
